title: T1DEXI
description: T1DEXI is a controlled-access CGM dataset released in 2022 and collected in the US. This dataset was collected from 497 participants with type 1 diabetes. The dataset was originally developed to support a real-world study of the acute glycemic effects of structured exercise in adults with type 1 diabetes. T1DEXI contains CGM data, insulin-related data, self-reported food intake logs, wearable activity tracker data such as step count, heart rate, sleep, and more from a Verily Study Watch, and data on structured exercise sessions including exercise type, duration, and intensity.

title: AI-READI
description: AI-READI is a controlled-access CGM dataset originally released in 2024 with v3.0.0 released in 2025. This dataset is being collected in the United States. AI-READI v3.0.0 was collected from 2280 participants with type 2 diabetes, prediabetes, and no diabetes. This dataset is actively being collected to support future generations of artificial intelligence/machine learning (AI/ML) research to provide critical insights into type 2 diabetes. AI-READI contains data from multiple modalities including CGMs, wearable activity trackers, electrocardiograms, environmental sensors, retinal photography, optical coherence tophography, fluorescence lifetime imaging ophthalmoscopy, clinical data, and more.

title: ShanghaiT2DM
description: ShanghaiT2DM is an open-access CGM dataset released in 2023 and collected in China. This dataset was collected from 100 participants with type 2 diabetes. The dataset was originally developed to support data-driven machine learning research in diabetes management. ShanghaiT2DM contains CGM data, insulin-related data such as basal rate, bolus, and insulin type, and self-reported meal logs.

title: Park2025
description: Park 2025 is an open-access CGM dataset released in 2025 and collected in the US. The dataset was originally developed to support research on the relationships between real-world lifestyle behaviors, glucose dynamics, and metabolic risk factors underlying the development of type 2 diabetes. Park 2025 contains CGM data, clinical data such as glycated hemoglobin, fasting glucose, fasting insulin, blood pressure, cholesterol, and more.

title: T1DEXIP
description: T1DEXIP is a controlled-access CGM dataset released in 2022 and collected in the US. This dataset was collected from 251 participants with type 1 diabetes. The dataset was originally developed to support analysis of the acute effects of real-world physical activity on glycemia in adolescents with type 1 diabetes. T1DEXIP contains CGM data, insulin-related data, self-reported food intake logs, wearable activity tracker data such as step count, heart rate, sleep, and more from a Verily Study Watch, and data on structured exercise sessions including exercise type, duration, and intensity.

title: T1DiabetesGranada
description: T1DiabetesGranada is a controlled-access CGM dataset released in 2024 and collected in Spain. This dataset was collected from 736 participants with type 1 diabetes. The dataset was originally developed to support overcoming data scarcity for AI models that characterize disease evolution and predict glucose levels in type 1 diabetes. T1DiabetesGranada  contains CGM data and clinical data such as glycated hemoglobin, cholesterol, triglycerides and more. No insulin, meal, or activity logs are included.

title: HUPA-UCM
description: HUPA-UCM is an open-access CGM dataset released in 2024 and collected in Spain. This dataset was collected from 25 participants with type 1 diabetes. This dataset was collected to support research on glucose prediction, hypoglycemia and hyperglycemia prediction, and to analyze the influence of quality and quantity of sleep and other variables on glucose trends. HUPA-UCM contains CGM data, insulin dose administration, and self-reported meal data in carbohydrate grams, and wearable activity tracker data such as steps, calories burned, heart rate, and sleep quantity and quality assessments.

title: BIGIDEAs
description: BIGIDEAs is an open-access CGM dataset originally released in 2022 and collected in the US. This dataset was collected from 16 participants with prediabetes and no diabetes. The dataset was originally developed to support testing whether wearable devices can detect early physiological changes and generate digital biomarkers for prediabetes and hyperglycemia risk. BIGIDEAs contains CGM data, meal logs, and wearable activity data from an Empatica E4 wristband, including data on accelerometry, skin temperature, heart rate, and more.

title: T1D-UOM
description: T1D-UOM is an open-access CGM dataset released in 2025 and collected in the UK. This dataset was collected from 17 participants with type 1 diabetes. The dataset was originally developed to support research on algorithm development to improve diabetes management, particularly in settings where automated insulin delivery (AID) technology is less accessible. T1D-UOM contains CGM data, insulin-related data such as basal and bolus insulin doses, nutritional information, including carbohydrates, protein, fat, and fiber content, and wearable activity data from Garmin Forerunner 45 such as step count, active calories, discover covered, and sleep data.

title: ShanghaiT1DM
description: ShanghaiT1DM is an open-access CGM dataset released in 2023 and collected in China. This dataset was collected from 12 participants with type 1 diabetes. The dataset was originally developed to support data-driven machine learning research in diabetes management. ShanghaiT1DM contains CGM data, insulin-related data such as basal rate, bolus, and insulin type, and self-reported meal logs.

title: Hall 2018
description: Hall 2018 is an open-access CGM dataset released in 2018 and collected in the US. This dataset was collected from 57 participants with type 2 diabetes, prediabetes, and no diabetes. The dataset was originally developed to support characterizing patterns of glucose dysregulation and the variability in glycemic responses to an identical nutrient challenge. Hall 2018 contains CGM data, standardized meals, and other clinical measurements such as fasting blood glucose, fasting insulin, steady-state plasma glucose, and more.

title: UCHTT1DM
description: UCHTT1DM is an open-access CGM dataset released in 2024 and collected in Chile. This dataset was collected from 20 participants with type 1 diabetes and no diabetes. The dataset was originally created to support research in glucose prediction using deep learning techniques. UCHTT1DM contains CGM data, insulin-relevant data, and wearable activity tracker data from Fitbit Charge 4 such as steps, heart rate, sleep, and more.

title: Bris-T1D Open
description: Bris-T1D Open is an open-access CGM dataset released in 2025 and collected in the UK. This dataset was collected from 24 young adults with type 1 diabetes. The dataset was originally developed to support research on blood glucose prediction, hypoglycaemia prediction, closed-loop algorithm development as well as broader mixed-methods research into the role of smartwatches in type 1 diabetes management. Bris-T1D Open contains CGM data, insulin pump data, wearable activity tracker data such as step count, calories burned, an heart rate, and qualitative interview and focus-group transcripts.

title: D1NAMO
description: D1NAMO is an open-access CGM dataset released in 2018 and collected in Switzerland. This dataset was collected from 9 participants with type 1 diabetes. The dataset was originally developed to support non-invasive type 1 diabetes management and detection of glycemic events from wearable physiological signals in real-life conditions. D1NAMO contains CGM data, insulin pump data, annotated food photographs, and wearable activity tracker data.

title: Colas 2019
description: Colas 2019 is an open-access CGM dataset released in 2019 and collected in Spain. This dataset was collected from 208 participants with no diabetes and type 2 diabetes. The dataset was originally developed to support prediction of type 2 diabetes onset in at-risk patients using complexity analysis of glucose time series. Colas 2019 contains CGM data.

title: CGMacros Libre
description: CGMacros is an open-access CGM dataset released in 2025 and collected in the US. This dataset was collected from 45 participants with type 2 diabetes, prediabetes, and no diabetes. The dataset was originally developed to support research on personalized nutrition and algorithmic approaches to automated diet monitoring. CGMacros contains CGM data from Dexcom & Freestyle Libre sensors, food macronutrient information, food photographs, physical activity and wearable activity tracker data from Fitbit Sense, demographic information, anthropometric measurements, blood biomarkers, and gut microbiome profiles.

title: CGMacros Dexcom
description: CGMacros is an open-access CGM dataset released in 2025 and collected in the US. This dataset was collected from 45 participants with type 2 diabetes, prediabetes, and no diabetes. The dataset was originally developed to support research on personalized nutrition and algorithmic approaches to automated diet monitoring. CGMacros contains CGM data from Dexcom & Freestyle Libre sensors, food macronutrient information, food photographs, physical activity and wearable activity tracker data from Fitbit Sense, demographic information, anthropometric measurements, blood biomarkers, and gut microbiome profiles.

title: DiaTrend
description: DiaTrend is a controlled-access CGM dataset released in 2023 and collected in the US. This dataset was collected from 54 participants with type 1 diabetes. The dataset was originally developed to support development of analytic solutions for diabetes management. DiaTrend contains CGM data, insulin pump data such as basal and bolus doses, and meal estimates from carbohydrate intake logs.

title: OhioT1DM
description: OhioT1DM is a controlled-access CGM dataset originally released in 2018 then updated in 2020 and collected in the US. This dataset was collected from 12 participants with T1D. The dataset was originally developed to support research in blood glucose level prediction. OhioT1DM contains CGM data, insulin pump data, self-reported data such as meals and stressors, and wearable tracker data such as steps, heart rate, and sleep.

title: AZT1D
description: AZT1D is an open-access CGM dataset released in 2025 and collected in the US. This dataset was collected from 25 participants with type 1 diabetes on automated insulin delivery (AID) systems. The dataset was originally developed to support research on artificial intelligence and machine learning applications aimed at improving clinical decision making and individualized care in type 1 diabetes. AZT1D contains CGM data, insulin pump and insulin administration data such as basal and bolus insulin doses, carbohydrate intake, and and device mode (regular, sleep, and exercise modes).

title: PhysioCGM
description: PhysioCGM is an open-access CGM dataset released in 2025 and collected in the US. This dataset was collected from 10 participants with type 1 diabetes. The dataset was originally developed to support research on the development of non-invasive methods for glucose monitoring. PhysioCGM contains CGM data and physiological data from electrocardiography (ECG), photoplethysmography (PPG), electrodermal activity (EDA) sensors, including skin temperature, accelerometry, heart rate, respiration rate, and more.
